title: Protocol Outcomes, Layers, Standards, and Evidence · Language Lens: Conformance Policy Extension
summary: A team says it built HTTP from scratch because one request works, but it cannot separate application semantics, wire syntax, transport behavior, implementation policy, or user and operator evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the conformance policy extension increment in protocol outcome, layer, authority, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate required conformance, optional tolerance, local policy, registered extensions, and unsupported behavior in parser decisions.
Use current RFC requirements, errata, Go documentation, and implementation tests as distinct authorities and resolve conflicts explicitly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Conformance Policy Extension · Theory Conformance Policy Extension
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 8957 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate model, wire, state, and authorized live-transfer evidence; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then truncate one content octet and classify incomplete framing without connection reuse. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate required conformance, optional tolerance, local policy, registered extensions, and unsupported behavior in parser decisions.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Conformance Policy Extension contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Conformance Policy Extension · Theory Conformance Policy Extension.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate required conformance, optional tolerance, local policy, registered extensions, and unsupported behavior in parser decisions. For Predict Conformance Policy Extension · Theory Conformance Policy Extension, keep Conformance Policy Extension connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Build Conformance Policy Extension evidence · Theory Conformance Policy Extension
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: httpproto-conformance-policy-extension-8j8fsd" that uses case 3821 to separate model, wire, state, and authorized live-transfer evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets or listeners, negotiate TLS, contact networks or proxies, capture packets, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Go 1.26 protocol transfer gates.

Build deterministic Go evidence to separate model, wire, state, and authorized live-transfer evidence; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate required conformance, optional tolerance, local policy, registered extensions, and unsupported behavior in parser decisions.
